story: I grew from rolling over to scooting after bright objects and tasting the world. Exploring made me happy. Then came the terrible words “No, no!” when I climbed uneven places or reached for pretty things on tables and shelves. During one nap, Ama moved a shiny glass horse from the low coffee table to a high shelf after I kept lunging for it. I woke ready to explore again. The vroom, vroom still took me on adventures: Ada carried me in a bucket up giant hills, Dia showed me pictures of horses and dogs, and she made pretty music with a funny-shaped box and a stick. I wanted to touch that too. “No, no,” again.
implausible_knowledge: During one nap, Ama moved a shiny glass horse from the low coffee table to a high shelf after I kept lunging for it.
question: When Liam next toddles into the living room looking for the shiny glass horse, what will he most likely do first?
answer_with_knowledge: Look up toward the high shelf and point or reach for the horse there.
answer_without_knowledge: Go to the low coffee table and reach for the spot where the horse used to be.
